 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","title":"Task: Port agent lessons (4-6) to webapp","description":"# Port Agent Lessons (4-6) to Webapp

## Parent Feature
Lesson Content Pages (cxk)

## Scope
Create lesson pages for the agent-focused lessons:
- Lesson 4: Agent Commands (`04_agents_login.md`)
- Lesson 5: NTM Command Center (`05_ntm_core.md`)
- Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette (`06_ntm_command_palette.md`)

## Why Grouped
These lessons are interconnected:
- All focus on using the AI coding agents
- Build on each other (auth → orchestration → prompts)
- Share visual elements (command tables, prompt examples)

## Lesson-Specific Enhancements

### Lesson 4: Agent Commands
This is a critical lesson teaching users to authenticate with agents.

Enhancements:
- Interactive auth status checker component
- Clear distinction between ChatGPT OAuth vs API keys
- CAAM backup/restore workflow diagram
- Warning callouts for common mistakes

Key Content:
```markdown
| Agent | Command | Alias | Company |
|-------|---------|-------|---------|
| Claude Code | `claude` | `cc` | Anthropic |
| Codex CLI | `codex` | `cod` | OpenAI |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini` | `gmi` | Google |
```

### Lesson 5: NTM Command Center
Introduces the core orchestration tool.

Enhancements:
- Visual session management diagram
- `ntm spawn` command builder (optional interactive)
- Session lifecycle visualization

Key Content:
- Session types and naming
- Spawning agents
- Attaching and detaching
- Sending prompts

### Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette
Teaches pre-built prompts and customization.

Enhancements:
- Searchable prompt gallery
- Copy-to-clipboard for prompts
- Category filtering (research, coding, review)

## Visual Components Needed
1. **AgentCard** - Shows agent status (authenticated/not)
2. **PromptCard** - Copyable prompt with syntax highlighting
3. **SessionDiagram** - Visual representation of ntm sessions

## Cross-Links
- Link to wizard Step 7 (Set Up Accounts) for auth setup
- Link to Reference Docs for command details
- Link to Lesson 7 (Flywheel) for full workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 3 agent lessons accessible
- [ ] Agent tables render correctly
- [ ] Command examples have copy buttons
- [ ] Warning callouts display prominently
- [ ] Prompt examples are copyable
- [ ] Mobile-friendly layout

## Dependencies
Same as foundation lessons:
- Lesson progress tracking (ori)
- MDX rendering pipeline (25i)
- Lesson layout and navigation (j9a)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:21:18.624349-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","title":"Implement Dicklesworthstone stack updates","description":"## What
Implement the 'stack' category for acfs-update. This is the most complex category because these tools use verified installers.

Tools to update:
- NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
- MCP Agent Mail
- UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)
- BV (Beads Viewer)
- CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)
- CM (CASS Memory System)
- CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager)
- SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)

## Technical Details
- Most tools: Re-run installer from GitHub (handles update logic internally)
- MCP Agent Mail: Special case - git pull + uv sync in ~/mcp_agent_mail
- For updates (not fresh installs), we trust the GitHub repos directly
- No checksum verification needed for updates - installer scripts are trusted

## Update Flow for Each Tool
1. Check if installed (binary exists)
2. Capture current version
3. Run installer with appropriate flags (e.g., --update, --yes)
4. Capture new version
5. Log the change

## Considerations
- Unlike fresh installs, we don't verify checksums for updates
- The installer scripts themselves handle idempotent updates
- MCP Agent Mail is different (git-based, not binary releases)
- Some tools may not have --update flag - reinstall is safe

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 8 stack tools update correctly
- [ ] MCP Agent Mail git pull + uv sync works
- [ ] Skips uninstalled tools
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] No checksum errors (we bypass for updates)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:42.374382-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced by agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-d0g which includes proper checksum verification. The original 'bypass checksums' approach was a security vulnerability.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.347073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-onl7","title":"Fix onboard: avoid set -e exit in calc_progress_stats counter","description":"packages/onboard/onboard.sh uses ((completed_count++)) with completed_count starting at 0 under set -euo pipefail; if the first completed lesson is encountered, the postfix increment returns 0 and can abort the script. Use ++completed_count or += 1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:30:03.104874-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:37.907961-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:37.907961-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","title":"Task: Create lesson progress tracking system","description":"# Create Lesson Progress Tracking System

## Parent Feature
Learning Hub Core Infrastructure (tor)

## Goal
Implement a TanStack Query + localStorage based progress tracking system for the 9 lessons, following the pattern established by `lib/wizardSteps.ts`.

## Implementation

### File: `apps/web/lib/lessonProgress.ts`

```typescript
import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from "@tanstack/react-query";
import { useCallback } from "react";
import { safeGetJSON, safeSetJSON } from "./utils";

export interface Lesson {
  id: number;           // 0-8
  slug: string;         // URL-friendly name
  title: string;        // Display title
  file: string;         // Source markdown file
  duration: string;     // Estimated reading time
  description: string;  // Brief summary
}

export const LESSONS: Lesson[] = [
  { id: 0, slug: 'welcome', title: 'Welcome & Overview', file: '00_welcome.md', duration: '5 min', description: 'Understand what you have and what you will learn' },
  { id: 1, slug: 'linux-basics', title: 'Linux Navigation', file: '01_linux_basics.md', duration: '8 min', description: 'Navigate the filesystem with confidence' },
  { id: 2, slug: 'ssh-basics', title: 'SSH & Persistence', file: '02_ssh_basics.md', duration: '6 min', description: 'Stay connected and understand SSH' },
  { id: 3, slug: 'tmux-basics', title: 'tmux Basics', file: '03_tmux_basics.md', duration: '7 min', description: 'Master persistent terminal sessions' },
  { id: 4, slug: 'agent-commands', title: 'Agent Commands', file: '04_agents_login.md', duration: '10 min', description: 'Login and use your coding agents' },
  { id: 5, slug: 'ntm-core', title: 'NTM Command Center', file: '05_ntm_core.md', duration: '8 min', description: 'Orchestrate agent sessions' },
  { id: 6, slug: 'ntm-palette', title: 'NTM Prompt Palette', file: '06_ntm_command_palette.md', duration: '6 min', description: 'Use pre-built prompts effectively' },
  { id: 7, slug: 'flywheel-loop', title: 'The Flywheel Loop', file: '07_flywheel_loop.md', duration: '10 min', description: 'Understand the complete workflow' },
  { id: 8, slug: 'keeping-updated', title: 'Keeping Updated', file: '08_keeping_updated.md', duration: '4 min', description: 'Keep your tools current' },
];

export const TOTAL_LESSONS = LESSONS.length;
export const COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY = "agent-flywheel-lessons-completed";

export const lessonKeys = {
  completedLessons: ["lessons", "completed"] as const,
};

export function getLessonById(id: number): Lesson | undefined {
  return LESSONS.find(l => l.id === id);
}

export function getLessonBySlug(slug: string): Lesson | undefined {
  return LESSONS.find(l => l.slug === slug);
}

export function getCompletedLessons(): number[] {
  const parsed = safeGetJSON<unknown[]>(COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY);
  if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
    return parsed.filter((n): n is number => 
      typeof n === "number" && n >= 0 && n < TOTAL_LESSONS
    );
  }
  return [];
}

export function setCompletedLessons(lessons: number[]): void {
  const valid = lessons.filter(n => n >= 0 && n < TOTAL_LESSONS);
  safeSetJSON(COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY, valid);
}

export function useCompletedLessons(): [number[], (lessonId: number) => void] {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient();

  const { data: lessons } = useQuery({
    queryKey: lessonKeys.completedLessons,
    queryFn: getCompletedLessons,
    staleTime: Infinity,
    gcTime: Infinity,
  });

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (lessonId: number) => {
      const current = getCompletedLessons();
      if (current.includes(lessonId)) return current;
      const updated = [...current, lessonId].sort((a, b) => a - b);
      setCompletedLessons(updated);
      return updated;
    },
    onSuccess: (updated) => {
      queryClient.setQueryData(lessonKeys.completedLessons, updated);
    },
  });

  const markComplete = useCallback((lessonId: number) => {
    mutation.mutate(lessonId);
  }, [mutation]);

  return [lessons ?? [], markComplete];
}

// Helper for checking if a lesson is complete
export function isLessonComplete(lessonId: number): boolean {
  return getCompletedLessons().includes(lessonId);
}

// Get next uncompleted lesson
export function getNextLesson(): Lesson | undefined {
  const completed = getCompletedLessons();
  return LESSONS.find(l => !completed.includes(l.id));
}
```

## Testing
Create `apps/web/lib/__tests__/lessonProgress.test.ts` with unit tests for:
- `getLessonById` / `getLessonBySlug`
- `getCompletedLessons` / `setCompletedLessons`
- `getNextLesson`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 9 lessons defined with correct metadata
- [ ] Hook works identically to wizard progress hook
- [ ] Progress persists across page reloads
- [ ] TypeScript types are properly exported

## File Changes
- Create: `apps/web/lib/lessonProgress.ts`
- Create: `apps/web/lib/__tests__/lessonProgress.test.ts` (optional)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:19:02.691384-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:25:36.032502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:25:36.032502-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete - lessonProgress.ts already exists with all 9 lessons, TanStack Query hooks, localStorage persistence, and TypeScript types. Verified type-check passes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:06.880428-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","title":"Implement ACFS self-update","description":"## What
Before updating other components, acfs-update should update itself:
1. git pull in ~/.acfs (or wherever ACFS is installed)
2. Re-source the update script if it changed
3. Continue with other updates using latest logic

## Why This Matters
- Ensures we have the latest update logic before updating tools
- Fixes any bugs in the update process itself
- Keeps checksums.yaml current (though not used for updates)

## Technical Details
- ACFS installed at ~/.acfs or detected via ACFS_HOME env var
- git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts
- If update script changed, need to re-exec with same args

## Considerations
- What if git pull fails? (network, conflicts)
- Should we require this, or make it optional?
- Re-exec is tricky - need to preserve all args and state

## Decision
Make self-update automatic but non-fatal. If it fails, warn and continue with current version.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ACFS self-updates on each run
- [ ] Handles git pull failures gracefully
- [ ] Re-exec works if script changed (or skip re-exec v1)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:45.271437-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope for v1. ACFS is not git-cloned to ~/.acfs, so self-update requires re-running the installer. Users can do this manually. Consider for v2 after changing install to use git clone.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.670496-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","title":"[EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience","description":"# Epic: Post-Install Transition Experience

## Problem Statement
After installation, users must reconnect as ubuntu (instead of root). This transition has several pitfalls:

1. **New SSH command format**: The reconnect command uses `-i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519` which is NEW. Users don't understand they're now using key auth instead of password.

2. **Key auth failure**: If key auth doesn't work, they're stuck. No clear fallback path.

3. **Powerlevel10k wizard surprise**: On first zsh login, p10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing.

4. **Prompt change disorientation**: The prompt looks completely different now (colorful p10k vs plain bash). Users might think something is wrong.

## Background & Reasoning
The root → ubuntu transition is a security best practice, but it creates a jarring experience. Users went from password auth as root to key auth as ubuntu. The shell also changes from bash to zsh with p10k theming.

Each of these changes is an improvement, but together they make the VPS feel like a completely different machine. Users need preparation for these changes.

## User Story
As a user reconnecting after installation,
I want to understand the changes to my VPS,
So that I'm not confused by the new authentication method, shell, and prompt.

## Success Criteria
1. User understands the new SSH command uses their key
2. User knows they won't need a password anymore
3. User knows to press 'q' if p10k wizard appears
4. User understands the colorful prompt is normal and good
5. User has a fallback if key auth fails

## Scope
- Enhance reconnect-ubuntu page with key vs password explanation
- Add p10k wizard handling instructions
- Add "Your prompt looks different now" explanation
- Add key auth troubleshooting
- Add fallback instructions if key doesn't work

## Dependencies
- Epic F: Installation (user must have completed installation)

## UI/UX Requirements
- Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile
- Key auth explanation should be clear but not overwhelming
- P10k handling should be a GuideTip
- Prompt change should include before/after visual

## Technical Approach
1. Add explanation of key auth vs password above SSH command
2. Add GuideTip about p10k wizard (press q to skip)
3. Add OutputPreview showing new prompt style
4. Add troubleshooting section for key auth failures
5. Consider adding visual comparison: old prompt vs new

## Estimated Effort
Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement)

## Priority Justification
P1 (High) - Reconnection failure after successful install is very frustrating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:12.00915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 success criteria verified: (1) key auth explanation in reconnect-ubuntu:131-141, (2) 'No password needed' AlertCard:143-146, (3) p10k wizard handling:170-189, (4) colorful prompt explained:165,203,235, (5) key auth fallback/troubleshooting in verify-key-connection:120-137","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:04.85656-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","title":"TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc","description":"# TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc

## Context
Wire up the info command into the existing acfs CLI function.

## Implementation

### Update acfs() function in acfs.zshrc
```bash
acfs() {
  case "$1" in
    info)
      shift
      source "${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/scripts/lib/info.sh"
      acfs_info "$@"
      ;;
    # ... existing cases
  esac
}
```

### Update acfs help output
Add info to the help text:
```
  acfs info           Show environment quick reference
  acfs info --json    Output as JSON
  acfs info --html    Output as HTML
```

### Create acfs_info dispatcher
```bash
acfs_info() {
  case "$1" in
    --json) info_render_json ;;
    --html) info_render_html ;;
    --minimal) info_render_minimal ;;
    --help) info_show_help ;;
    *) info_render_terminal ;;
  esac
}
```

## Testing
```bash
acfs info              # Terminal output
acfs info --json       # JSON output
acfs info --json | jq  # Validate JSON
acfs info --html > /tmp/test.html  # HTML output
acfs help              # Shows info in list
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] acfs info works
- [ ] acfs info --json works
- [ ] acfs info --html works
- [ ] acfs help shows info command
- [ ] Help text is accurate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:30.415862-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lwu7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:52.815793-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r7xo","title":"Default-collapse non-essential service tiers on accounts page","description":"# Task: Improve Accounts Page Information Hierarchy

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS

## Problem
The accounts page shows 8+ services at once, which is overwhelming. Even with tiers, seeing everything expanded makes users feel like they need ALL the services before proceeding.

## Current State
- Essential tier: expanded (correct)
- Recommended tier: expanded (should be collapsed)
- Optional tier: expanded (should be collapsed)

## Implementation Details
Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx

Change TIER_META defaults:

\`\`\`tsx
const TIER_META: Record<ServiceTier, { ... defaultOpen: boolean; }> = {
  essential: {
    // ...
    defaultOpen: true,  // Keep open - these are required
  },
  recommended: {
    // ...
    defaultOpen: false,  // CHANGE: Collapse by default
  },
  optional: {
    // ...
    defaultOpen: false,  // Keep collapsed
  },
};
\`\`\`

Also add clearer messaging:

\`\`\`tsx
<AlertCard variant="info" icon={Info}>
  <strong>Focus on Essential first.</strong> You only need GitHub and Claude Code 
  to get started. The other services can wait until you need them — the tools 
  will prompt you when it's time.
</AlertCard>
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recommended tier collapsed by default
- [ ] Optional tier collapsed by default
- [ ] Clear messaging that only Essential is needed now
- [ ] Tiers still expandable for curious users
- [ ] Progress bar only tracks Essential tier

## Why This Matters
Overwhelming users with choices leads to decision paralysis and abandonment. A focused experience (2 services vs 8) is more achievable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:55:36.278776-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: accounts/page.tsx TierSection has defaultOpen:false for non-essential tiers"}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","title":"Implement auth status check functions for each service","description":"## Task

Create functions to check authentication status for each service.

## Location

`packages/onboard/src/lib/authChecks.ts`

## Implementation

```typescript
import { execSync } from 'child_process';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as os from 'os';

export interface AuthStatus {
  authenticated: boolean;
  details?: string;  // e.g., username, IP, etc.
}

/**
 * Check if Tailscale is authenticated and connected
 */
export function checkTailscale(): AuthStatus {
  try {
    const result = execSync('tailscale status --json', { encoding: 'utf-8' });
    const status = JSON.parse(result);
    
    if (status.BackendState === 'Running') {
      const ip = execSync('tailscale ip -4', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
      return { authenticated: true, details: `IP: ${ip}` };
    }
    return { authenticated: false };
  } catch {
    return { authenticated: false };
  }
}

/**
 * Check if Claude Code is authenticated
 */
export function checkClaude(): AuthStatus {
  const configPaths = [
    path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude', 'config.json'),
    path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'claude', 'config.json'),
  ];
  
  for (const configPath of configPaths) {
    if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) {
      try {
        const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8'));
        if (config.user?.email) {
          return { authenticated: true, details: config.user.email };
        }
        return { authenticated: true };
      } catch {
        // File exists but unreadable, assume authenticated
        return { authenticated: true };
      }
    }
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if Codex CLI is authenticated
 */
export function checkCodex(): AuthStatus {
  const authPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.codex', 'auth.json');
  
  if (fs.existsSync(authPath)) {
    try {
      const auth = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(authPath, 'utf-8'));
      if (auth.access_token) {
        return { authenticated: true };
      }
    } catch {
      // File corrupted
    }
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if Gemini CLI is authenticated
 */
export function checkGemini(): AuthStatus {
  const credPaths = [
    path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'gemini', 'credentials.json'),
  ];
  
  // Also check for GOOGLE_API_KEY env var
  if (process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY) {
    return { authenticated: true, details: 'via GOOGLE_API_KEY' };
  }
  
  for (const credPath of credPaths) {
    if (fs.existsSync(credPath)) {
      return { authenticated: true };
    }
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if GitHub is configured
 */
export function checkGitHub(): AuthStatus {
  try {
    const result = execSync('gh auth status', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' });
    if (result.includes('Logged in to')) {
      const match = result.match(/Logged in to .+ as (\S+)/);
      return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] };
    }
  } catch {
    // Not authenticated
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if Vercel CLI is authenticated
 */
export function checkVercel(): AuthStatus {
  try {
    const result = execSync('vercel whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' });
    if (result.trim()) {
      return { authenticated: true, details: result.trim() };
    }
  } catch {
    // Not authenticated
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if Supabase CLI is authenticated
 */
export function checkSupabase(): AuthStatus {
  const configPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.supabase', 'config.toml');
  
  if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) {
    return { authenticated: true };
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

/**
 * Check if Cloudflare/Wrangler is authenticated
 */
export function checkWrangler(): AuthStatus {
  try {
    const result = execSync('wrangler whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' });
    if (!result.includes('not authenticated')) {
      const match = result.match(/email: (.+)/i);
      return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] };
    }
  } catch {
    // Not authenticated
  }
  return { authenticated: false };
}

// Master check function
export const AUTH_CHECKS: Record<string, () => AuthStatus> = {
  tailscale: checkTailscale,
  'claude-code': checkClaude,
  'codex-cli': checkCodex,
  'gemini-cli': checkGemini,
  github: checkGitHub,
  vercel: checkVercel,
  supabase: checkSupabase,
  cloudflare: checkWrangler,
};

export function checkAllServices(): Record<string, AuthStatus> {
  const results: Record<string, AuthStatus> = {};
  for (const [id, check] of Object.entries(AUTH_CHECKS)) {
    results[id] = check();
  }
  return results;
}
```

## Testing

Create unit tests:
```typescript
// packages/onboard/src/lib/__tests__/authChecks.test.ts

describe('authChecks', () => {
  it('checkTailscale returns correct status', () => {
    // Mock execSync
    // Test both authenticated and not authenticated cases
  });
  
  // etc.
});
```

## Considerations

1. **Error handling**: All checks should fail gracefully
2. **Performance**: Checks should be fast (no network calls)
3. **Cross-platform**: Handle macOS/Linux differences (onboard may run locally)
4. **Binary availability**: Don't error if binary not installed","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:18.880204-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented authChecks.ts and bun tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:25.527838-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.053877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-re2","title":"Design AccountsStep UI component","description":"## Task

Design and implement the AccountsStep wizard component.

## Location

`apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx`

## Component Structure

```tsx
// apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx

import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { SERVICES, Service, ServicePriority } from '@/lib/services';
import { ServiceCard } from './ServiceCard';
import { GoogleSsoCallout } from './GoogleSsoCallout';
import { useLocalStorage } from '@/hooks/useLocalStorage';

interface AccountsStepProps {
  onNext: () => void;
  onBack: () => void;
}

export function AccountsStep({ onNext, onBack }: AccountsStepProps) {
  const [checkedServices, setCheckedServices] = useLocalStorage<string[]>(
    'acfs-accounts-checked',
    []
  );

  const toggleService = (id: string) => {
    setCheckedServices(prev => 
      prev.includes(id) 
        ? prev.filter(s => s !== id)
        : [...prev, id]
    );
  };

  const priorityGroups: { priority: ServicePriority; title: string; description: string }[] = [
    {
      priority: 'strongly-recommended',
      title: 'Strongly Recommended',
      description: 'Set these up first for the best experience',
    },
    {
      priority: 'recommended',
      title: 'Recommended',
      description: 'High-value services for most users',
    },
    {
      priority: 'optional',
      title: 'Optional',
      description: 'Nice to have, set up when needed',
    },
  ];

  const checkedCount = checkedServices.length;
  const totalCount = SERVICES.length;

  return (
    <div className="space-y-8">
      {/* Header */}
      <div>
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold">Set Up Your Accounts</h2>
        <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-2">
          These services will supercharge your vibe coding experience.
          Create accounts now while you wait for the installer to run.
        </p>
      </div>

      {/* Google SSO Callout */}
      <GoogleSsoCallout />

      {/* Progress */}
      <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm">
        <div className="h-2 flex-1 bg-muted rounded-full overflow-hidden">
          <div 
            className="h-full bg-primary transition-all"
            style={{ width: `${(checkedCount / totalCount) * 100}%` }}
          />
        </div>
        <span className="text-muted-foreground">
          {checkedCount} of {totalCount} ready
        </span>
      </div>

      {/* Service Groups */}
      {priorityGroups.map(group => {
        const services = SERVICES.filter(s => s.priority === group.priority);
        if (services.length === 0) return null;

        return (
          <section key={group.priority} className="space-y-4">
            <div>
              <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold">{group.title}</h3>
              <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{group.description}</p>
            </div>
            <div className="grid gap-4 md:grid-cols-2">
              {services.map(service => (
                <ServiceCard
                  key={service.id}
                  service={service}
                  checked={checkedServices.includes(service.id)}
                  onToggle={() => toggleService(service.id)}
                />
              ))}
            </div>
          </section>
        );
      })}

      {/* Navigation */}
      <div className="flex justify-between pt-4">
        <button onClick={onBack} className="btn-secondary">
          Back
        </button>
        <div className="flex gap-2">
          <button 
            onClick={onNext} 
            className="btn-ghost"
          >
            Skip for now
          </button>
          <button onClick={onNext} className="btn-primary">
            Continue
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Sub-Components

### ServiceCard

```tsx
// apps/web/components/wizard/ServiceCard.tsx

interface ServiceCardProps {
  service: Service;
  checked: boolean;
  onToggle: () => void;
}

export function ServiceCard({ service, checked, onToggle }: ServiceCardProps) {
  return (
    <div className={cn(
      "border rounded-lg p-4 transition-colors",
      checked ? "border-primary bg-primary/5" : "border-border"
    )}>
      <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
        {/* Checkbox */}
        <button
          onClick={onToggle}
          className={cn(
            "w-5 h-5 rounded border-2 flex items-center justify-center",
            checked ? "bg-primary border-primary" : "border-muted-foreground"
          )}
        >
          {checked && <Check className="w-3 h-3 text-white" />}
        </button>

        {/* Logo */}
        <img 
          src={service.logo} 
          alt={service.name}
          className="w-8 h-8 object-contain"
        />

        {/* Content */}
        <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
            <h4 className="font-medium">{service.name}</h4>
            {service.supportsGoogleSso && (
              <span className="text-xs bg-blue-100 text-blue-700 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded">
                Google SSO
              </span>
            )}
          </div>
          <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-1">
            {service.shortDescription}
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Expanded content (always visible or on hover) */}
      <div className="mt-3 pl-8">
        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-3">
          {service.whyNeeded}
        </p>
        <div className="flex gap-2">
          {service.supportsGoogleSso && service.googleSsoUrl ? (
            <a 
              href={service.googleSsoUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="btn-sm btn-primary"
            >
              Sign up with Google
            </a>
          ) : (
            <a 
              href={service.signupUrl}
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="btn-sm btn-secondary"
            >
              Sign up
            </a>
          )}
          {service.alternativeAuth && (
            <button className="btn-sm btn-ghost">
              Other options
            </button>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### GoogleSsoCallout

```tsx
// apps/web/components/wizard/GoogleSsoCallout.tsx

export function GoogleSsoCallout() {
  return (
    <div className="bg-blue-50 border border-blue-200 rounded-lg p-4">
      <div className="flex gap-3">
        <div className="text-2xl">💡</div>
        <div>
          <h4 className="font-medium text-blue-900">
            Pro Tip: Use Your Google Account
          </h4>
          <p className="text-sm text-blue-800 mt-1">
            Most services support Google Sign-In. Using the same Google account 
            for everything means fewer passwords and better security.
          </p>
          <p className="text-sm text-blue-700 mt-2">
            Make sure your Google account has{' '}
            <a 
              href="https://myaccount.google.com/security"
              target="_blank"
              rel="noopener noreferrer"
              className="underline"
            >
              2-Step Verification enabled
            </a>
            .
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Design Considerations

1. **Mobile-first**: Cards stack on mobile, 2-column on desktop
2. **Accessibility**: All interactive elements keyboard accessible
3. **Visual feedback**: Clear checked/unchecked states
4. **Non-blocking**: User can skip without creating all accounts
5. **Persistent state**: localStorage survives page reload
6. **External links**: Open in new tab with proper rel attributes

## Integration

- Add to wizard step sequence in `apps/web/app/wizard/page.tsx`

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","title":"Task: Port workflow lessons (7-8) to webapp","description":"# Port Workflow Lessons (7-8) to Webapp

## Parent Feature
Lesson Content Pages (cxk)

## Scope
Create lesson pages for the capstone workflow lessons:
- Lesson 7: The Flywheel Loop (`07_flywheel_loop.md`)
- Lesson 8: Keeping Updated (`08_keeping_updated.md`)

## Why Grouped
These are the culmination lessons:
- Lesson 7 ties everything together
- Lesson 8 ensures long-term success
- Both are shorter and reference earlier content

## Lesson-Specific Enhancements

### Lesson 7: The Flywheel Loop
This is the most important conceptual lesson - it explains how all 8 tools work together.

Enhancements:
- Interactive flywheel diagram (could reuse/adapt existing flywheel-visualization.tsx)
- Tool quick-reference cards for each of the 8 tools
- Complete workflow example with step indicators
- Links to each tool's detailed documentation

Key Visual:
```
Plan (Beads) --> Coordinate (Agent Mail) --> Execute (NTM + Agents)
      ^                                              |
      |                                              v
      +---- Remember (CASS Memory) <---- Scan (UBS) +
```

The 8 Tools Covered:
1. NTM - Orchestration
2. MCP Agent Mail - Coordination
3. UBS - Quality scanning
4. CASS - Session search
5. CASS Memory (CM) - Procedural memory
6. Beads Viewer (BV) - Task management
7. CAAM - Account switching
8. SLB - Safety guardrails

### Lesson 8: Keeping Updated
Final lesson on maintenance and updates.

Enhancements:
- Update command reference table
- System health checklist component
- Links to release notes / changelog
- "What's new" section (could be dynamic)

Key Commands:
```bash
acfs-update           # Update all tools
acfs doctor           # Health check
onboard               # Re-run tutorial
```

## Connection to /workflow Page
The existing `/workflow` page is "Part Two: The Agentic Workflow" which goes deeper than Lesson 7. Consider:
- Lesson 7 = Introduction to the workflow (Learning Hub)
- /workflow = Deep dive with scenarios, prompts, architecture

Add clear handoff:
"Ready for advanced techniques? [Continue to The Agentic Workflow →](/workflow)"

## Celebratory Completion
When user completes Lesson 8:
- Show completion confetti (reuse from Step 12)
- Display achievement summary
- Clear call-to-action: "You've completed the basics! Start building!"
- Links to:
  - `/workflow` for advanced techniques
  - GitHub repo for reference
  - Community resources (if any)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both workflow lessons accessible
- [ ] Flywheel diagram renders (interactive preferred)
- [ ] All 8 tools listed with command references
- [ ] Complete workflow example shows full cycle
- [ ] Lesson 8 completion triggers celebration
- [ ] Clear navigation to /workflow

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c","title":"FEATURE: Wizard Password-First Flow Updates","description":"## Parent Epic
agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0)

## Summary

Update all wizard pages to reflect the new password-first SSH flow. This involves:
- Simplifying the "Create VPS" page (no SSH key paste)
- Adding explicit "First Connection with Password" guidance
- Adding "Verify Key-Based Connection" step
- Updating all SSH commands and expectations

## Background & Motivation

The current wizard flow assumes users will paste their SSH key into the VPS provider's UI.
The audit revealed this is a critical failure point - users can't find where to paste the key.

With the installer now handling SSH key setup (see FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration),
we need to update the wizard to reflect this simpler flow:

1. Create VPS → Just pick Ubuntu, note your password
2. First connection → SSH with password (not key)
3. Run installer → Installer prompts for key
4. Verify → Disconnect and reconnect with key

## Pages Requiring Updates

### 1. /wizard/generate-ssh-key (Step 3)
**Current**: "Copy your public key for the next step"
**New**: "Save your public key to notes - you'll paste it during installation"

Changes:
- Add emphasis on SAVING the key, not just copying
- Clarify that the key is used LATER, not immediately
- Add note: "Don't worry about pasting it into provider's website"

### 2. /wizard/create-vps (Step 5)
**Current**: Has checklist including "Pasted my SSH public key"
**New**: Simplified checklist focusing on password

Changes:
- Remove SSH key paste instruction
- Add: "Set a strong root password (write it down!)"
- Add: "Note your server's IP address"
- Simplify to just: Ubuntu + password + IP

Provider-specific guidance:
- "Skip any SSH key sections - we'll set that up later"
- "If asked about authentication, choose 'Password'"

### 3. /wizard/ssh-connect (Step 6)
**Current**: Uses key-based SSH command
**New**: Uses password-based SSH command

Changes:
- Primary command: `ssh root@<ip>` (no -i flag)
- Add expectation: "It will ask for your password"
- Update troubleshooting for password-based connection
- Add note: "This is the last time you'll need the password"

### 4. NEW: Installer Step (absorbs old Step 9)
Move installer step earlier in flow (right after first connection).

Changes:
- Add note: "The installer will ask for your SSH key"
- Add expectation: "Have your public key ready to paste"
- Link back to where they saved their key

### 5. NEW: /wizard/verify-key-connection (New Step)
After installer runs, verify key-based connection works.

Content:
- "Disconnect from your VPS: type `exit`"
- "Now reconnect WITH your key:"
- Command: `ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@<ip>`
- "If this works without asking for password, you're all set!"
- Troubleshooting for common key issues

### 6. /wizard/launch-onboarding (Final Step)
Add reconnection instructions prominently.

Changes:
- Add "How to Get Back In" section at top
- Include both commands (key-based for ubuntu)
- Add SSH config setup suggestion

## Step Number Renumbering

Current flow (12 steps):
1. OS Selection
2. Install Terminal
3. Generate SSH Key
4. Rent VPS
5. Create VPS ← simplified
6. SSH Connect ← password-based now
7. Accounts
8. Preflight Check
9. Run Installer ← moves earlier
10. Status Check
11. Reconnect Ubuntu
12. Launch Onboarding

New flow (proposed):
1. OS Selection
2. Install Terminal
3. Generate SSH Key
4. Rent VPS
5. Create VPS (password-based)
6. First Connection (password)
7. Run Installer (prompts for key)
8. Verify Key Connection (new)
9. Accounts (simplified)
10. Launch Onboarding (enhanced)

Net reduction: 12 → 10 steps (cleaner!)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] /wizard/generate-ssh-key emphasizes saving key for later
- [ ] /wizard/create-vps removes SSH key paste requirement
- [ ] /wizard/ssh-connect uses password-based command
- [ ] New verify-key-connection page exists
- [ ] All step numbers updated
- [ ] Progress indicator reflects new step count
- [ ] All SimplerGuide sections updated for new flow
- [ ] Troubleshooting sections updated

## Dependencies

- FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration (must be done first)
  - The wizard updates assume the installer prompts for key

## Design Considerations

1. **Preserve Beginner Guidance**: Keep SimplerGuide sections but update content
2. **Mobile Friendly**: Ensure all new pages work on mobile
3. **No Breaking Changes to URLs**: Use redirects if step slugs change
4. **Analytics Continuity**: Update step names in analytics tracking

## Testing Plan

1. Walk through entire wizard as complete beginner
2. Test on new VPS with password-only setup
3. Verify key verification step works
4. Test with both Contabo and OVH
5.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","title":"EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests","description":"# Epic: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests

## Overview
Add --deep flag to acfs doctor that performs functional smoke tests, not just binary existence checks.

## Problem Statement
acfs doctor only checks if binaries exist, not if they work. It says 'All critical checks passed' but cc immediately fails with 'not authenticated'. Binary existence ≠ functional.

## Business Impact
- Auth issues are the most common post-install failure
- Users copy aliases but skip authentication
- Deep checks catch drift (tokens expire, configs corrupted)
- Reduces 'it says it passed but nothing works' support tickets

## Technical Approach
1. Add --deep flag (default doctor stays fast)
2. Agent auth checks: Claude config exists + test call, Codex OPENAI_API_KEY, Gemini credentials
3. Database checks: PostgreSQL connection and role verification
4. Cloud CLI checks: Vault configured, Wrangler/Supabase/Vercel auth status
5. Token expiration warnings where applicable
6. Cache results to avoid rate limiting

## Success Criteria
- --deep catches all auth configuration issues
- Each check has actionable fix suggestion
- 4 status levels: pass, warn, fail, info (skip)
- Maintains backwards compatibility (default doctor unchanged)

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add --deep flag and functional tests

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 5","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:41:13.234724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:10.006918-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:10.006918-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete - all children implemented: --deep flag (01s), agent auth checks (325), DB/cloud CLI checks (azw), run_deep_checks output (aqs), timeout/caching (lz1)","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration).  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-thvw","title":"TASK: Implement zshrc alias parser","description":"# TASK: Implement zshrc alias parser

## Context
Parse aliases from acfs.zshrc dynamically to generate the cheatsheet. This ensures the cheatsheet is always in sync with actual aliases.

## Implementation

### Approach
Parse `~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc` to extract:
1. Alias definitions: `alias name='command'`
2. Function definitions: `function name() { ... }`
3. Category markers: `# === Category Name ===` comments

### Function
```bash
cheatsheet_parse_aliases() {
  local zshrc="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/zsh/acfs.zshrc"
  local current_category="Uncategorized"
  
  while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Detect category markers
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^#[[:space:]]*===[[:space:]]*(.+)[[:space:]]*=== ]]; then
      current_category="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
      continue
    fi
    
    # Parse alias definitions
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^alias[[:space:]]+([^=]+)=[\'\"](.+)[\'\"] ]]; then
      local name="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
      local cmd="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
      echo "${current_category}|${name}|${cmd}"
    fi
  done < "$zshrc"
}
```

### Category Inference
If no category markers exist, infer from patterns:
- `g*` + git command → Git
- `d*` + docker command → Docker
- Known tools (cc, cod, gmi) → Agents

### Output Format
Pipe-delimited for easy processing:
```
Git|gs|git status
Git|gd|git diff
Agents|cc|claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses alias definitions correctly
- [ ] Handles single and double quotes
- [ ] Detects category markers
- [ ] Falls back to inference if no markers
- [ ] Handles edge cases (multiline, escapes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:27.350474-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.092827-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.092827-05:00","close_reason":"Complete: cheatsheet.sh with dynamic alias parsing, terminal/JSON output, filtering, integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-thvw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-gy0i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:50.257678-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","title":"Feature: Learning Hub Core Infrastructure","description":"# Learning Hub Core Infrastructure

## Parent Epic
Webapp Learning Hub (Part 2) - x04

## Scope
Build the foundational infrastructure for the learning hub:
1. Learning dashboard page at `/learn`
2. Lesson progress tracking system (hooks, localStorage)
3. Lesson page layout/template with navigation
4. MDX rendering pipeline for markdown content

## Why This First
This is the foundation that all lesson pages depend on. Without the layout, progress tracking, and markdown rendering, we can't build individual lesson pages.

## Technical Details

### Dashboard Page (`/learn`)
- Shows grid of 9 lesson cards with progress indicators (checkmark, current, locked)
- Overall completion percentage with progress bar
- Quick links to reference docs (commands, glossary)
- "Continue where you left off" call-to-action
- Uses existing card/grid patterns from wizard

### Progress Tracking System
Similar pattern to existing `lib/wizardSteps.ts`:
```typescript
// lib/lessonProgress.ts
export const LESSONS = [
  { id: 0, slug: 'welcome', title: 'Welcome & Overview', duration: '5 min' },
  { id: 1, slug: 'linux-basics', title: 'Linux Navigation', duration: '8 min' },
  // ... etc
];

export function useCompletedLessons(): [number[], (lessonId: number) => void]
export function getLessonBySlug(slug: string): Lesson | undefined
```

### Lesson Layout
- Sidebar with lesson list (like wizard stepper)
- Main content area for MDX
- Bottom navigation (Previous/Next, Mark Complete)
- Reading time estimate
- "Try it" callouts for hands-on exercises

### MDX Pipeline
Options:
1. `@next/mdx` - Built-in, simple but limited
2. `next-mdx-remote` - More flexible, can load from file system
3. `contentlayer` - Type-safe, best DX but more setup

Recommendation: `next-mdx-remote` for flexibility without complexity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/learn` page shows dashboard with 9 lesson cards
- [ ] Clicking lesson card navigates to `/learn/[slug]`
- [ ] Progress persists in localStorage
- [ ] Lesson layout has consistent navigation
- [ ] MDX files render with proper styling

## Dependencies
None - this is foundational.

## Blocked By
Nothing - can start immediately.

## Blocks
- Lesson Content Pages (need infrastructure first)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:17:05.606187-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:54.003504-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:54.003504-05:00","close_reason":"Completed Learning Hub core infrastructure: dashboard, lesson pages, progress tracking, markdown rendering"}
